message:
chore(deps): bump asupersync 0.2.9 → 0.3.0 and switch from local path dep to crates.io

Replaces the hardcoded `path = "/data/projects/asupersync"` dependency
in both Rust helper tools with the freshly published crates.io release.
Path deps only worked on the author's workstation (the absolute
/data/projects layout); crates.io lets a fresh clone build without a
sibling checkout. v0.3.0 (2026-04-21) ships 2500+ commits since 0.2.9,
including a large test-compile hygiene pass, the digest-0.11 /
sha1+sha2+hmac dependency wave, three real production concurrency
fixes, and substantial new conformance/golden coverage — all of which
improve the fixtures these two binaries produce.

- tools/fixture_capture/Cargo.toml:
  asupersync = { path = "/data/projects/asupersync", features = ["test-internals"] }
  → asupersync = { version = "0.3.0", features = ["test-internals"] }
- tools/rust_fuzz_target/Cargo.toml: same swap.
- Both Cargo.lock files regenerated via `cargo update` to reflect the
  crates.io source. The update also advances transitive deps that
  asupersync 0.3.0 pulled forward (libc, prost, semver, sha1/sha2,
  signal-hook, tracing-subscriber, typenum, uuid, wasip2,
  wasm-bindgen*, web-sys, wit-bindgen).
